potential_missing.source_sentence: This setup allows users to smoothly move between browsing products, viewing details, managing their basket, and completing their purchase in a flexible and user-friendly way.

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall allow users to browse products through a catalog.
The system shall allow users to browse products using an index, either alphabetically or by category.
The system shall enable users to review the items in their basket at any time.
The system shall allow users from the basket to either return to the catalog or proceed to checkout.
The system shall allow users to return to the catalog from the checkout page to make changes before completing the process.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: When they enter the catalog, they can view items directly or explore different ways of browsing.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall provide users with multiple browsing options upon entering the catalog.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: Once they find something interesting, they can select a product to view its details.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow users to select a product for detailed viewing once an item of interest is found.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable users from the basket to either return to the catalog or proceed to checkout.
hallucinations.reason: There is no mention in the source text about returning to the catalog from the basket; it only mentions going back to the catalog.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow users to browse products through a catalog.
duplicates.requirement_b: In an online shopping system, users can browse products through a catalog.
duplicates.reason: These requirements are essentially the same and should be considered duplicates.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable users to switch between browsing options based on their preferences.
ambiguities.reason: It is unclear if 'switching' implies a dynamic change in real-time or just the ability to choose from predefined options.

final_assessment_policy: moderate: needs_review if extraction/rewriting failed, no rewritten requirements were produced, exact duplicate rewritten requirements were detected, or the LLM validator reports 6 or more total issues across missing requirements, hallucinations, duplicates, and ambiguities
